historyMessageContext.description: Number of previous messages passed as context to the LLM

llmId.description: ID of the active LLM model

chatStart.description: Controls whether a new conversation starts with the AI bot ("ai") or routes immediately to a human agent ("agent")

GeneralSettingsInput.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are applied.

llmModelId.description: UUID of an active LLM model from the `websiteLlmModels` table

maxMessagesPerConversation.description: Only settable when limitMessagesPerConversation is true

chatStart.description: Set to "agent" to route new conversations directly to a human agent

get.description:
Returns all chatbots the authenticated user personally has access to, grouped by account — across every account they belong to. No `accountId` is required.

**Access rules per account:**
- `SUPER_ADMIN` or account owner → sees all chatbots in that account, with their per-chatbot role resolved via a left join on `chatbot_members`
- Regular member → sees only chatbots they are explicitly added to in `chatbot_members`

Results are **Redis-cached for 10 minutes** per user (`user:{userId}:chatbots`). Use this endpoint to build a cross-account chatbot dashboard for the logged-in user.

> **Contrast with [Get All Chatbots in Account](#operation/getAllChatbotsInAccount):** that endpoint is scoped to a single account, requires `accountId`, returns a flat unfiltered list, and has no caching.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing token, expired JWT, revoked session, or user no longer exists

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — user has sent more than 500 requests in the current 60-second window

message.example: Rate limit exceeded. Too many requests — please wait before trying again.

example.message: Rate limit exceeded. Too many requests — please wait before trying again.

500.description: Internal server error — unhandled exception or infrastructure failure (DB crash, Redis down, unexpected throw). Caught by the global `errorHandler` middleware. In `NODE_ENV=development` the response also includes a `stack` field with the full stack trace.

stack.description: Stack trace — present only in NODE_ENV=development

get.description:
Fetches all chatbots in a specific account — no per-user filtering applied. Requires `accountId` as a path parameter. The authenticated user must have `CHATBOT_VIEW` permission in that account.

Returns a flat list of every chatbot in the account along with the user's account-level role. Results are **not cached**.

> **Contrast with [Get My Chatbots](#operation/getMyChatbots):** that endpoint requires no `accountId`, works across all accounts the user belongs to, applies role-based per-chatbot filtering, and is Redis-cached for 10 minutes.

get.description:
Fetch the appearance/UI settings for a chatbot. Returns all visual customisation fields — colours, icons, layout, watermark, font, etc.

Requires `CHATBOT_VIEW` permission. The `chatbotId → accountId` mapping is resolved from a Redis cache (TTL 20 min) so no extra DB hit is needed.

> Returns **404** if appearance settings have never been created for this chatbot — call `POST` first.

403.description: Forbidden — user lacks `CHATBOT_VIEW` permission for this chatbot

error.message: Insufficient permissions to perform this action

404.description: Not Found — appearance settings have not been created for this chatbot yet

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — more than 100 requests per second from this IP

error.message: Too many requests — please wait before trying again

500.description: Internal server error — unhandled exception or infrastructure failure (DB crash, Redis down, unexpected throw). Caught by the global `errorHandler` middleware. In `NODE_ENV=development` the `error.details` object also includes a `stack` field.

post.description:
Create appearance/UI settings for a chatbot. Can only be called **once per chatbot** — returns `409` if settings already exist. Use `PATCH` to update.

Icon fields (`botIconSrc`, `userIconSrc`, `agentIconSrc`, `bubbleIconSrc`, `watermarkBrandIcon`) accept either a URL string **or** a base64-encoded image (`data:image/...`). Base64 values are uploaded to ImageKit automatically and replaced with the resulting CDN URL.

Requires `EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS` permission.

schema.description: Create appearance settings with optional file uploads for icons. All fields are optional.

botIconSrc.description: Icon file for the bot avatar

userIconSrc.description: Icon file for the user avatar

agentIconSrc.description: Icon file for the human agent avatar

bubbleIconSrc.description: Icon file for the chat bubble launcher

watermarkBrandIcon.description: Icon file for the watermark brand logo

error.message: Insufficient permissions to perform this action

409.description: Conflict — appearance settings already exist for this chatbot; use PATCH to update

error.message: Appearance settings already exist for this chatbot. Use update instead.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — more than 100 requests per second from this IP

error.message: Too many requests — please wait before trying again

500.description: Internal server error — unhandled exception or infrastructure failure (DB crash, Redis down, unexpected throw). Caught by the global `errorHandler` middleware. In `NODE_ENV=development` the `error.details` object also includes a `stack` field.

patch.description:
Partially update the appearance/UI settings for a chatbot. Send only the fields you want to change — all fields are optional.

Icon fields (`botIconSrc`, `userIconSrc`, `agentIconSrc`, `bubbleIconSrc`, `watermarkBrandIcon`) accept either a URL string **or** a base64-encoded image (`data:image/...`). Base64 values are uploaded to ImageKit and replaced with the CDN URL before saving.

Returns `400` if the request body contains no recognised fields. Returns `404` if appearance settings don't exist yet — call `POST` first.

Invalidates the `chatbot:init_config:{chatbotId}` Redis cache key on success.

Requires `EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS` permission.

schema.description: Update appearance settings with optional file uploads for icons. All fields are optional.

post.description:
Sets a specific instruction as the active one for the chatbot, deactivating all others. You can identify the instruction by `instructionId` or `title`. If neither matches an existing instruction **and** both `title` and `instruction` text are provided, a new instruction is created and immediately activated.

Requires `EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS` permission.

instructionId.description: ID of an existing instruction to activate. If provided, title/instruction are ignored for lookup.

title.description: Title of an existing instruction to find by name, or the title for a new instruction if none is found.

instruction.description: Required only when creating a new instruction (i.e. no match found by instructionId or title).

Too many requests — please wait before trying again

patch.description:
Updates the general settings for a chatbot. If no settings row exists yet, one is created (upsert). Only provided fields are applied.

This is also the endpoint to update `chatStart` (chat mode). Set `chatStart` to `"ai"` or `"agent"` to control whether conversations start with the AI or a human agent.

Invalidates the `chatbot:init_config:{chatbotId}` Redis cache. A notification email is sent to the user who made the change.

> **Note:** `maxMessagesPerConversation` can only be set when `limitMessagesPerConversation` is `true`.

Requires `EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS` permission.

200.description: Updated (or created if settings row didn't exist)

400.description: Bad Request — no valid fields, or maxMessagesPerConversation set while limitMessagesPerConversation is disabled

value.message: limitMessagesPerConversation must be enabled before setting maxMessagesPerConversation.

403.description: Forbidden — missing EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS permission or plan doesn't allow conversation limits

error.message: Insufficient permissions to perform this action

Batch-updates the status of one or more threads. Accepts an array of update objects, each containing a `threadId` and the fields to change. Broadcasts `thread:updated` events via Socket.IO.

Requires `CHATBOT_MANAGE` permission.

patch.description:
Toggles the escalated flag on a thread (true → false or false → true) and inserts a system message recording the change. Broadcasts `thread:updated` and `message:new` events via Socket.IO. Fires an outbound webhook when escalating (false → true).

Requires `CHATBOT_MANAGE` permission.

Requires `CHATBOT_MANAGE` permission.

post.description:
Archives the current thread and creates a fresh one for the same visitor. Logs `/reset` and a bot confirmation message in the old thread, then broadcasts `thread:reset` via Socket.IO. Returns the new thread along with conversation starters and follow-up prompts.

Requires `CHATBOT_MANAGE` permission.

patch.description:
Updates the general settings for a chatbot. If no settings row exists yet, one is created (upsert). Only provided fields are applied.

> **Note:** `maxMessagesPerConversation` can only be set when `limitMessagesPerConversation` is `true`.

Requires `EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS` permission.

schema.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are applied.

maxMessagesPerConversation.description: Only settable when limitMessagesPerConversation is true

patch.description:
Updates one or more of the five chatbot icons. You can provide:
1. File uploads via multipart/form-data (new recommended way)
2. URLs or base64-encoded images (`data:image/...`) via JSON

Base64 images and file uploads are automatically uploaded to ImageKit and the resulting URL is stored.

Appearance settings must already exist (create them via the appearance endpoint first).

Invalidates the `chatbot:init_config:{chatbotId}` Redis cache.

Requires `EDIT_CHATBOT_SETTINGS` permission.

schema.description: All fields are optional. Upload icon files as multipart form fields. Only provided fields are updated.

botIconSrc.description: Icon file for the bot avatar

userIconSrc.description: Icon file for the user avatar

agentIconSrc.description: Icon file for the human agent avatar

bubbleIconSrc.description: Icon file for the chat bubble launcher icon

watermarkBrandIcon.description: Icon file for the watermark brand logo

schema.description: All fields are optional. Pass a URL or base64 image. Only provided fields are updated.

botIconSrc.description: URL or base64 image for the bot avatar

userIconSrc.description: URL or base64 image for the user avatar

agentIconSrc.description: URL or base64 image for the human agent avatar

bubbleIconSrc.description: URL or base64 image for the chat bubble launcher icon

watermarkBrandIcon.description: URL or base64 image for the watermark brand logo

agentIconSrc.description: URL of the uploaded/stored human agent avatar icon
